page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It does not contain any GPUs.

Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 97227.26 and strong cached read-only memory bandwidth at 3792.89 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in software compilation with Clang and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 985.0 and weak single-threaded gzip compression. LLM inference performance is generally weak to poor, particularly for text generation.

The bare-metal architecture offers high resource density and direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring large memory footprints and fast local storage. The combination of 512 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD, and strong Redis SET performance makes this server highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The server lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 97227.26 and strong cached memory bandwidth of 3792.89 GB/sec. It also excels in static web serving and Redis SET operations, achieving 74.12 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving workloads that benefit from direct hardware access and local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-throughput web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB DDR4 memory and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong multi-core performance and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Achieves high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and software compilation
